diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Depressed consciousness progressing to coma is the defining decompensation feature and mandates emergency care; carer-reportable via telehealth.
Coma is shared with toxidromes and must prompt an overdose screen, but is non-specific on its own.

symptoms.symptom_name: Severe hypoventilation with cyanosis (very slow shallow breaths, blue lips)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Blunted central respiratory drive with hypoxia/hypercapnia is a cardinal life-threatening feature; carer-observable dusky lips and slow breathing are admissible telehealth proxies.
Respiratory depression is shared with opioid toxicity — the reason an overdose must be screened for.

symptoms.symptom_name: Ice-cold to the touch, found in a cold environment (profound hypothermia)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Profound hypothermia from collapsed thermogenesis is a hallmark of myxoedema coma; the carer can report that she is cold all over even where blankets can't explain it.
The cold house contributes, but does not by itself account for the facial oedema and metabolic picture.

symptoms.symptom_name: Known hypothyroidism with thyroxine stopped ~5 days ago

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Withdrawal of thyroid hormone replacement in a dependent hypothyroid patient is the central precipitant and reframes coma as decompensated hypothyroidism — carer-reportable and pivotal.
An intercurrent infection is a classic metabolic stressor that precipitates crisis in a decompensating hypothyroid patient.
The infection may itself require treatment and can coexist with the endocrine emergency.

symptoms.symptom_name: Puffy non-pitting facial/periorbital oedema with coarse dry skin and thin hair

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Myxoedematous facies (puffy face/eyelids) with coarse dry skin is a specific marker of severe hypothyroidism; the puffy face and dry skin are video-observable/carer-reportable, though the non-pitting quality needs examination.

symptoms.symptom_name: Generalised flaccidity — completely limp and not moving

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Global symmetrical flaccidity fits diffuse metabolic depression and argues against a focal stroke.
Symmetrical global flaccidity is not the focal, lateralised deficit expected of a stroke.
Low-output cardiogenic shock from depressed contractility — a sealed clinician measurement; the AI Doctor infers shock from carer-observable signs, not a measured BP.

symptoms.symptom_name: Severe bradycardia (HR 42 bpm) with muffled heart sounds

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Bradycardia and muffled heart sounds reflect hypothyroid cardiac depression (and possible pericardial effusion) — clinician-obtained, sealed from telehealth; the bradycardia also distinguishes this from tachycardic sepsis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Measured profound hypothermia (core temperature 32.5 C) and RR 8 / SpO2 88%

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: The measured core temperature, respiratory rate and oxygen saturation quantify the emergency — clinician-obtained values sealed from telehealth; the AI relies on the carer's report of cold, slow breathing and blue lips.
The delayed relaxation phase of reflexes is characteristic of hypothyroidism — a clinician examination finding, sealed from telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Low bedside capillary blood glucose (3.2 mmol/L)

investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Hypoglycaemia (a treatable contributor to coma and a feature of myxoedema coma) is missed.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Hypoglycaemia from reduced hepatic gluconeogenesis is a recognised feature of myxoedema coma and a treatable cause of reduced consciousness — a bedside test not available here as the household has no glucometer, so sealed.

symptoms.symptom_name: Anticipated markedly elevated TSH with low free T4 (pending phlebotomy)

investigation_details.investigation_name: Serum TSH and free T4 (with sodium and cortisol)
investigation_details.expected_result: Markedly elevated TSH with low free T4; possible hyponatraemia; cortisol to exclude concurrent adrenal insufficiency
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: The biochemical confirmation of myxoedema coma is missed; treatment nonetheless begins on clinical grounds.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A markedly elevated TSH with low free T4 is the confirmatory signature of decompensated primary hypothyroidism — a hospital investigation, never available in real time via telehealth.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Profound hypothermia + altered mental status/coma + non-pitting facial oedema + the anticipated markedly elevated TSH/low T4 together are the sealed answer-key tetrad that defines myxoedema coma.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Emergency ambulance transfer (000) — do not delay for investigations
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Coma with respiratory failure, profound hypothermia and shock needs emergency airway/ventilatory support and resuscitation; no test should delay escalation.
Confirms decompensated primary hypothyroidism (markedly elevated TSH, low T4) — obtained in hospital, not via telehealth, and must not delay treatment.
Identifies and allows correction of hypoglycaemia as a treatable contributor to coma.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Serum cortisol, electrolytes (sodium), septic screen and cultures
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Excludes concurrent adrenal insufficiency (glucocorticoid is given empirically), detects hyponatraemia, and identifies the precipitating infection for parallel treatment.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Rapid active external rewarming (hot bath / electric blanket) at home
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Contraindicated — rapid/active rewarming causes peripheral vasodilation and can precipitate fatal circulatory collapse; only gentle passive rewarming with blankets is safe pending paramedics.
